name: Stryper
description:
'''Stryper''' is an American [[Christian metal]] band. The group's lineup consists of [[Michael Sweet]] (lead vocals, guitar), [[Oz Fox]] (guitar, backing vocals), [[Perry Richardson]] (bass, backing vocals), and [[Robert Sweet (musician)|Robert Sweet]] (drums).

Formed in 1981 as Roxx Regime, the band soon changed their musical message to reflect their [[Christianity|Christian beliefs]], and changed their name to Stryper in 1983. They went on to become the first overtly Christian heavy metal band to gain acceptance in the mainstream. Signing with independent label [[Enigma Records]], they released their debut [[extended play]], ''[[The Yellow and Black Attack]]'', in 1984. In the mid-1980s, Stryper enjoyed their most successful period beginning with the release of ''[[To Hell with the Devil]]'', which was [[Music recording sales certification|certified]] platinum by the [[Recording Industry Association of America|RIAA]]. Stryper went on to release two more gold-certified albums before disbanding in 1993.

In 2003, Stryper came out of retirement for a reunion tour and subsequently signed a multi-album contract with Big3 Records in 2005. In 2013, they signed a multi-album deal with [[Frontiers Records]] and have since released ''[[Second Coming (Stryper album)|Second Coming]]'' in 2013, which includes 14 re-recorded songs from their first three albums, ''[[No More Hell to Pay]]'' in 2013, ''[[Fallen (Stryper album)|Fallen]]'' in 2015, ''[[God Damn Evil]]'' in 2018, ''[[Even the Devil Believes]]'' in 2020, ''The Final Battle'' in 2022, ''[[When We Were Kings (Stryper album)|When We Were Kings]]'' and ''To Hell with the Amps'', their first acoustic album, in 2024, and ''[[The Greatest Gift of All (Stryper album)|The Greatest Gift of All]]'' in 2025. 

Over the course of their career, Stryper has placed 13 albums in the top 10 of ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]''{{'}}s [[Top Christian Albums]] chart, with five releases peaking at number two and one release — ''Even the Devil Believes'' — reaching number one. The band was described by the ''[[Los Angeles Times]]'' as the best-selling rock band in [[Orange County, California|Orange County]] from 1984 to 1990, with five albums during that period selling near or above 500,000 copies.
